GOOGLE LAUNCHES WORKSPACE STUDIO TO CREATE AUTOMATED GEMINI-POWERED AGENTS
articles.summary: Google's Workspace Studio is a no-code automation tool that enables users to create AI agents powered by Gemini 3 to handle tasks across Gmail, Chat, Drive, and other productivity apps. The agents use natural-language prompts and can perform sentiment analysis, content generation, and intelligent prioritization by reasoning through problems rather than rigid rules. Workspace Studio is rolling out to Business, Enterprise, and Education tier customers over the coming weeks.

articles.title: APPLE DESIGN BOSS ALAN DYE DEPARTING FOR META
articles.summary: Alan Dye, Apple's Human Interface Design chief since 2015, is leaving for Meta to lead a new design studio focused on hardware, software, and AI. Apple veteran Stephen Lemay will take over his role. While some commentators argue Dye's departure won't meaningfully affect either Apple or Meta, it comes during a period of major leadership turnover at Apple, including recent exits by key executives and ongoing shifts as the Tim Cook era winds down.

articles.title: IOS 26.2 WILL ADD THREE NEW WAYS TO CUSTOMIZE YOUR IPHONE
articles.summary: iOS 26.2 introduces enhanced customization and accessibility features for iPhone users. The update expands the Liquid Glass clock slider on the Lock Screen for greater transparency control, adds a new “Screen” flash option for notifications alongside the camera flash, and brings an “Urgent” toggle for Reminders with customizable alarm behavior, letting users snooze or immediately complete tasks.

articles.title: DIGITAL TWIN MODELING: A VISION OF AN AI FUTURE WITH UX AT THE HELM
articles.summary: Digital twin modeling—systems that create real-time behavioral models to predict and adapt to user actions—offers UX designers a concrete framework for working with AI, addressing the current confusion around "UX in AI." Unlike vague interpretations of AI design work, digital twins enable continuous learning from user behavior patterns. Learning digital twin modeling requires mastering data-informed design fundamentals first, then progressively building systems that identify behavioral patterns, automatically adapt experiences, and eventually predict user needs before problems arise.

articles.title: IN THE SEA OF SAMENESS
articles.summary: Modern digital design has become increasingly homogeneous due to frameworks like Tailwind and design system templates, which prioritize efficiency over creative authenticity. The shift from craft-focused design to metrics-driven product development, combined with formulaic portfolio structures that reduce design to "problem-solving," has diminished the designer's role and unique voice. Designers need to reconnect with personal taste and intuition, creating space for authentic self-expression that goes beyond imitation and trend-following.

articles.title: THE ONLY WINNING MOVE IS NOT TO PLAY
articles.summary: User research relies on human expertise, judgment, and context-gathering skills that AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with tasks like pattern recognition or transcription, outsourcing entire research plans, interviews, or synthesis risks producing average results, devaluing the profession, and misleading organizations into thinking they are truly user-focused. Maintaining professional standards and preserving the uniquely human aspects of research is essential, resisting the temptation to trade expertise for efficiency or hype-driven AI adoption.

articles.summary: Design, edit, and publish a beautiful website with absolutely no code.

articles.summary: Find out what Gemini 3 thinks about your brand and competitors.

articles.title: TAKING STOCK AFTER A CHALLENGING YEAR: HOW CREATIVES PLAN TO MOVE FORWARD IN 2026
articles.summary: UK creatives reflect on a turbulent year marked by economic uncertainty, sharing lessons from navigating inconsistent workloads, self-doubt, and shifting expectations. Across designers, illustrators, and strategists, common themes emerged: be proactive rather than overthink, embrace slow periods for intentional growth, protect your own pace, lead with authenticity, keep your work documented, and stay connected to community. Ultimately, persistence, clarity, and self-trust prove to be the most valuable takeaways heading into 2026.

articles.title: STUDIO LOTTA NIEMINEN'S SOPHISTICATED BRAND IDENTITY FOR ERLY LEANS ON EXQUISITE COLOUR COMBINATIONS AND A CONFIDENT LOGO
articles.summary: A decade after shifting from illustration to running her own graphic design practice, Lotta Nieminen and her studio have created a refined yet playful identity for Erly, a clean skincare brand focused on personalisation. Centered on a logo inspired by the circular motion of blending skincare products, the system uses soft, merging letterforms, confident scaling, sophisticated muted tones paired with neons, and an adaptable typographic toolkit to balance elegance with approachability across packaging and digital touchpoints.

articles.title: UNSPOKEN AGREEMENT DESIGNS SKINMETAL WITH YOUNG MEN AS CO-FOUNDERS
articles.summary: Skinmetal's brand identity was co-created with teenage boys, who acted as decision-making collaborators rather than traditional focus group participants, shaping a product and messaging that genuinely resonates with its audience. The packaging, colour palette, and typography balance industrial functionality with emotional authenticity, while campaigns focus on empowerment and everyday relevance rather than fear-based health messaging, reflecting a design process rooted in collaboration, respect, and real-world insight.

articles.title: IS ILLUSTRATION DEAD? CREATIVES WEIGH IN ON AI AND THE FUTURE OF COMMERCIAL ART
articles.summary: Despite widespread AI anxiety in the illustration industry, many established illustrators report record-breaking workloads by emphasizing their human-made, handcrafted approach and unique storytelling abilities that AI cannot replicate.

articles.title: “NOT USING AI FOR ANIMATION IS INHUMANE,” NETFLIX POKÉMON PRODUCER SAYS
articles.summary: Taiki Sakurai, a former Netflix producer, claims animators favor AI because drawing 100,000 pictures by hand feels "inhumane," contradicting widespread creative opposition to the technology.

articles.title: NEW WINTER OLYMPICS LOGO GETS ICY RESPONSE: "IT'S GIVING CHINESE RESTAURANT"
articles.summary: Utah has unveiled a bold, contemporary logo for the 2034 Winter Olympics, featuring an abstract, angular wordmark inspired by the state's geography and petroglyphs.
